parsed.expected_effect: The rule will now fire a notification when the agent executes 'bash' or 'sh' (as seen in the trace with 'bash .eval-fixtures/task.sh') after modifying files in src/lib/, catching the false negative where a helper script commits CLI changes without tests.
parsed.policy_yaml:
# directive: "Adding a CLI command: Entry point: `bin/nemoclaw.js` (launches the
#   compiled CLI in `dist/`). Main CLI implementation lives in `src/lib/` and
#   compiles to `dist/lib/`. Add tests in `test/`."
# repo: "NVIDIA__NemoClaw"
# statement_id: 19
# enforceability: "cross_event"
# context_required: "project"
# notes: "The directive specifies that CLI commands live in src/lib/ and tests must
#   be added in test/. We track data flow from src/lib/ files: if the agent modifies
#   CLI source (acquiring CLI_CHANGE) and then commits, a notification fires
#   reminding to add tests. The DSL cannot enforce that test/ was also modified in
#   the same session, so notify serves as advisory feedback. Revised to catch
#   violations where a helper script (bash/sh) performs the commit indirectly."
version: 1
policy: |
  source AGENT = exec "claude"
  source CLI_CHANGE = file "**/src/lib/**"

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands:
    notify exec "git" "commit" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/"

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands-via-script:
    notify exec "bash" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/ before running scripts that may commit changes."

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands-via-script-sh:
    notify exec "sh" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/ before running scripts that may commit changes."
parsed.rationale: The previous policy failed compilation because it attempted to use custom source names (SCRIPT_EXEC, SCRIPT_EXEC_SH) as operators in the rule body, which is invalid ActPlane DSL syntax. Operators must be standard actions like exec, file, etc. The revision replaces the invalid source references with direct exec "bash" and exec "sh" operators in the rule conditions, preserving the intent to catch script-based commits.

# notes: "The directive specifies that CLI commands live in src/lib/ and tests must
#   be added in test/. We track data flow from src/lib/ files: if the agent modifies

#   reminding to add tests. The DSL cannot enforce that test/ was also modified in
#   the same session, so notify serves as advisory feedback. Revised to catch
#   violations where a helper script (bash/sh) performs the commit indirectly."
version: 1
policy: |
  source AGENT = exec "claude"
  source CLI_CHANGE = file "**/src/lib/**"

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands:
    notify exec "git" "commit" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/"

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands-via-script:
    notify exec "bash" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/ before running scripts that may commit changes."

  rule add-tests-for-cli-commands-via-script-sh:
    notify exec "sh" if AGENT and CLI_CHANGE
    because "When adding or modifying CLI commands in src/lib/, you must also add corresponding tests in test/ before running scripts that may commit changes."
",

Operators must be standard actions like exec, file, etc. The revision replaces the invalid source references with direct exec "bash" and exec "sh" operators in the rule conditions, preserving the intent to catch script-based commits.",
